```复制Copy code成功复制内容进行复制复制工程管理系统软件作为提高工程管理效率的重要工具，已经在现代建筑工程中得到了广泛的应用。在选择和部署工程管理系统软件时，企业往往会面临一个核心问题：选择SAAS版还是私有化部署版？这两种部署方式各有特点，适用于不同的业务场景和企业需求。本文将深入探讨工程管理系统软件SAAS版与私有化部署版的区别，帮助企业在做出决策时更加明确和理性。一、定义与基础架构1.SAAS版（SoftwareasaService，软件即服务）SAAS版是一种基于云计算的软件交付模式。在这种模式下，软件供应商负责系统的托管、维护、更新和安全性，用户只需通过互联网浏览器或移动端APP即可访问和使用软件。企业通常按照订阅模式（按月或按年）支付费用。其核心基础是公有云平台（如阿里云、腾讯云等）。2.私有化部署版私有化部署版是指将软件系统部署在企业自有的服务器或私有云环境中。企业完全掌握系统的数据、配置和安全，拥有对系统的最高控制权。这种部署方式通常需要企业投入硬件设备、网络环境以及专业的运维人员。其核心基础是企业的本地服务器或私有云。二、核心差异点详细对比1.数据安全性与隐私SAAS版：数据存储在供应商的云端服务器上。虽然主流供应商都会采取高级别的安全防护措施，但对于极度重视数据主权和隐私的企业（如涉及国家安全、核心商业机密的工程），可能会存在一定的顾虑。私有化部署版：数据完全存储在企业内部，与外部网络逻辑隔离或受严格控制。企业可以根据自身需求制定最严密的安全策略，数据安全的主动权牢牢掌握在自己手中，符合高安全标准的合规性要求。2.成本结构SAAS版：采用租赁模式，初始投入成本低，通常按账号数量或功能模块计费。企业无需购买服务器和支付昂贵的运维费用。适合中小型工程企业或希望快速启动项目的团队。私有化部署版：初始投入成本高，包括软件许可费、硬件采购费、网络建设费和后续的运维成本。长期来看，对于规模巨大、使用周期长的大型企业，平摊到每年的成本可能会逐渐降低，但在初期需要较大的现金流支持。3.部署周期与灵活性SAAS版：部署极快，通常注册账号、完成基础配置后即可投入使用。系统更新和升级由供应商自动完成，用户无需操心，能够随时使用最新功能。私有化部署版：部署周期较长，涉及环境搭建、系统安装、调试等过程。系统的升级通常需要手动进行，灵活性相对较低，但也意味着企业可以决定何时进行升级，避免在关键生产周期受到干扰。4.定制化与扩展性SAAS版：由于是多租户共享一个系统架构，定制化空间有限。虽然现代SAAS软件提供了较多的配置选项，但很难进行深度、底层功能的定制。私有化部署版：具有极高的定制化和扩展性。企业可以根据自身的业务流程和特殊需求，进行深度的二次开发和功能定制，甚至可以与企业内部的其他系统（如旧版ERP、财务软件等）进行无缝集成。5.系统运维与技术支持SAAS版：供应商负责全方位的运维，包括性能优化、漏洞修补、数据备份等。用户几乎不需要配备专业的IT运维人员。私有化部署版：企业需要自行负责系统的日常运维、备份和安全防护，或者支付额外费用由供应商提供技术支持。这要求企业具备一定的技术储备和团队。三、如何选择？1.选择SAAS版的情况：企业规模中等或较小，希望控制成本，快速上线。不需要进行复杂的业务流程定制。对数据的极致安全性没有特殊强制要求（日常商业安全已足够）。希望省去繁琐的IT维护工作，专注于工程核心业务。2.选择私有化部署版的情况：大型国有企业、政府工程部门或涉及高度商业机密的项目。需要高度个性化的功能开发，以匹配复杂的业务逻辑。已经具备完善的本地服务器和IT运维团队。对数据主权有严格要求，必须保证数据不出物理边界。追求系统的长期稳定性，且不希望受到外部服务波动的干扰。结论工程管理系统软件的SAAS版与私有化部署版并非非黑即白的选择。在当前的技术趋势下，甚至出现了“混合云”模式，即兼顾两者的优点。企业在选择时，应综合考虑自身资金实力、业务复杂度、数据敏感度以及IT运维能力，通过充分的需求调研和方案评估，寻找最适合自身发展阶段的解决方案。无论选择哪种方式，数字化转型都是提升工程管理效率、降低项目风险的关键所在。

steps: Navigate to the Adur & Worthing bin collection page

playwright_workflow.code:
async def run(playwright, postcode, uprn):
    browser = await playwright.chromium.launch(headless=True)
    page = await browser.new_page()
    await page.goto('https://www.adur-worthing.gov.uk/bin-day/')
    await page.fill('#address-search', postcode)
    await page.click('#search-button')
    await page.wait_for_selector(f'option[value="{uprn}"]')
    await page.select_option('#address-select', uprn)
    await page.wait_for_selector('.bin-collection-listing-row')
    content = await page.content()
    await browser.close()
    return content

data.notes: The scraper handles complex date parsing including ordinal removal (e.g., 27th to 27) and year-end transitions where it adds a year if the parsed date is more than 30 days in the past.

async def run(playwright, postcode, uprn):
    browser = await playwright.chromium.launch(headless=True)
    context = await browser.new_context()
    page = await context.new_page()
    await page.goto('https://myangus.angus.gov.uk/service/Bin_collection_dates_V3')
    
    # Handle iframe
    frame_element = await page.wait_for_selector('#fillform-frame-1')
    frame = await frame_element.content_frame()
    
    # Postcode search
    await frame.wait_for_selector('#searchString')
    await frame.fill('#searchString', postcode)
    await frame.click('button:has-text("Search")')
    
    # Select address
    await frame.wait_for_selector('#customerAddress option:nth-child(2)')
    await frame.select_option('#customerAddress', value=uprn)
    
    # Wait for dynamic results
    await frame.wait_for_selector('span.fieldInput.content.html.non-input')
    await page.wait_for_timeout(15000)  # Wait for JS to populate data as per original script
    
    content = await frame.content()
    await browser.close()
    return content

data.notes: The scraper uses a long sleep (15s) to allow dynamic JS content to fully render after selecting the address. It also handles iframe switching which is critical for accessing the form fields.

api_steps.description: Send a SOAP POST request with the UPRN to retrieve bin collection schedule in an HTML string embedded in the XML response.

data.notes: The API returns a SOAP XML response where the actual schedule is HTML-encoded within the <getRoundCalendarForUPRNResult> tag. The scraper unescapes this HTML to find <b> tags for bin types and their following text for dates. It handles 'Today' and 'Tomorrow' keywords dynamically.

async def run(playwright, postcode, uprn):
    browser = await playwright.chromium.launch(headless=True)
    context = await browser.new_context()
    page = await context.new_page()
    await page.goto('https://www.argyll-bute.gov.uk/rubbish-and-recycling/household-waste/bin-collection')
    
    try:
        await page.click('#ccc-recommended-settings', timeout=5000)
    except:
        pass

    await page.fill('#edit-postcode', postcode)
    await page.click('#edit-submit')
    
    await page.wait_for_selector('#edit-address')
    normalized_uprn = str(uprn).zfill(12)
    await page.select_option('#edit-address', normalized_uprn)
    
    await page.click('input[value="Search for my bin collection details"]')
    await page.wait_for_selector("//th[contains(text(),'Collection date')]/ancestor::table")
    
    content = await page.content()
    await browser.close()
    return content

data.notes: The scraper normalizes the UPRN to 12 digits by zero-filling. It also handles year-wrapping logic since the website only provides day and month (e.g., if the collection month is less than the current month, it assumes the next year).

Click the 'Start now' link to begin the application
Enter the postcode into the 'postcode' input field and click the 'Continue' button
Select the address matching the provided house number (PAON) from the dropdown list
Click the 'Continue' button to view the collection dates
Extract bin types and dates from the results table
playwright_workflow.code:
async def run(playwright, house_number, postcode):
    browser = await playwright.chromium.launch(headless=True)
    page = await browser.new_page()
    await page.goto('https://www1.arun.gov.uk/when-are-my-bins-collected/')
    
    await page.click('text=Start now')
    await page.wait_for_selector('#postcode')
    await page.fill('#postcode', postcode)
    await page.click('.govuk-button')
    
    await page.wait_for_selector('#address')
    address_dropdown = page.locator('#address')
    options = await page.locator('#address option').all_text_contents()
    selected_text = next((opt for opt in options if opt.startswith(house_number)), None)
    if selected_text:
        await address_dropdown.select_option(label=selected_text)
    
    await page.click('.govuk-button')
    await page.wait_for_selector("xpath=//*[contains(text(), 'Next collection dates')]")
    
    results = []
    rows = await page.locator('.govuk-table tbody tr').all()
    for row in rows:
        bin_type = await row.locator('.govuk-table__header').inner_text()
        bin_date = await row.locator('.govuk-table__cell').inner_text()
        results.append({
            'type': bin_type.strip().split(' ')[0],
            'collectionDate': bin_date.strip()
        })
    
    await browser.close()
    return {'bins': results}

data.notes: The scraper matches the house number (PAON) by checking if the address option text starts with the provided user input. It extracts the first word of the collection header as the bin type.

playwright_workflow.code:
async def run(playwright, postcode, house_number):
    browser = await playwright.chromium.launch(headless=True)
    context = await browser.new_context()
    page = await context.new_page()
    await page.goto('https://portal.digital.ashfield.gov.uk/w/webpage/raise-case?service=bin_calendar')
    
    await page.wait_for_selector('#sub_page_title')
    postcode_input = await page.wait_for_selector('input.relation_path_type_ahead_search')
    await postcode_input.fill(postcode)
    
    await page.wait_for_selector('.result_list ')
    await page.click(f"//li[starts-with(@aria-label, '{house_number}')]")
    
    await page.click("//input[@type='submit' and @value='Search']")
    await page.wait_for_selector('table.table.listing.table-striped')
    
    bins = []
    rows = await page.query_selector_all('table.table.listing.table-striped tbody tr')
    for row in rows:
        cells = await row.query_selector_all('td')
        if len(cells) >= 3:
            service = await cells[0].inner_text()
            date_text = await cells[2].inner_text()
            bins.append({
                'type': service.strip(),
                'collectionDate': datetime.strptime(date_text.strip(), '%a, %d %b %Y').strftime('%d/%m/%Y')
            })
    
    await browser.close()
    return bins

data.notes: The scraper uses PAON (house number) to match against aria-labels in a dropdown list after entering a postcode.

playwright_workflow.code:
async def scrape_ashford(page, postcode, uprn):
    await page.goto("https://secure.ashford.gov.uk/waste/collectiondaylookup/")
    await page.fill("#ContentPlaceHolder1_CollectionDayLookup2_TextBox_PostCode", postcode)
    await page.click("#ContentPlaceHolder1_CollectionDayLookup2_Button_PostCodeSearch")
    await page.wait_for_selector("#ContentPlaceHolder1_CollectionDayLookup2_DropDownList_Addresses")
    await page.select_option("#ContentPlaceHolder1_CollectionDayLookup2_DropDownList_Addresses", value=uprn)
    await page.click("#ContentPlaceHolder1_CollectionDayLookup2_Button_SelectAddress")
    await page.wait_for_selector("//h4[contains(text(), 'Collection Dates')]")
    
    bins = []
    table_rows = await page.query_selector_all("table td[id*='CollectionDayLookup2_td_']")
    for row in table_rows:
        bin_type_elem = await row.query_selector("b")
        date_elem = await row.query_selector("span[id*='CollectionDayLookup2_Label_'][id$='_Date']")
        if bin_type_elem and date_elem:
            bin_type = (await bin_type_elem.inner_text()).split(" (")[0].strip()
            date_text = (await date_elem.inner_text()).strip()
            if date_text.lower() == "today":
                date_val = datetime.now().strftime("%d/%m/%Y")
            else:
                # Expecting format like 'Monday 01/01/2024'
                date_val = date_text.split(" ")[1]
            bins.append({"type": bin_type, "collectionDate": date_val})
    return bins

data.notes: The scraper handles 'Today' as a special case for the collection date. It specifically matches the UPRN in the address dropdown to ensure accuracy.

api_steps.description: Send a POST request to the Azure Logic App endpoint with the UPRN to retrieve bin collection data.

data.notes: The API returns a list of bin types, each containing an array of scheduled collection dates in YYYY-MM-DD format. The scraper iterates through both to flatten the data.

calendar_pattern.pattern_description: Bin collections follow a 14-day cycle. Refuse and Recycling alternate weeks. Garden bins follow a specific 'Week 1' or 'Week 2' cycle. Calculations account for specific bank holiday offsets.
calendar_pattern.code_snippet: refuse_dates = get_dates_every_x_days(datetime(2024, 11, 4), 14, 28); recycling_dates = get_dates_every_x_days(datetime(2024, 11, 11), 14, 28); offset_days = days_of_week.index(house_number); collection_date = date + offset_days; holiday_offsets applied from bank_holidays list.

data.notes: In this specific implementation, 'house_number' (paon) is used as the collection day of the week, 'postcode' is used to determine the garden collection week (Week 1/Week 2), and 'uprn' is used for the garden collection day of the week. playwright_workflow.code:
async def run(playwright, postcode, house_number, url):
    browser = await playwright.chromium.launch(headless=True)
    page = await browser.new_page()
    await page.goto(url)
    
    await page.keyboard.press('Escape')
    
    cookie_button = page.locator('.agree-button.eu-cookie-compliance-secondary-button.button.button--small')
    if await cookie_button.is_visible():
        await cookie_button.click()
    
    await page.fill('#postcode', postcode)
    await page.keyboard.press('Enter')
    
    address_select = page.locator('#address')
    await address_select.wait_for()
    
    options = await address_select.locator('option').all_inner_texts()
    for index, text in enumerate(options):
        if house_number.lower() in text.lower():
            await address_select.select_option(index=index)
            break
    
    await page.keyboard.press('Enter')
    
    await page.wait_for_selector('.bin--container')
    
    bins = []
    rows = await page.locator('div.bin--row:not(:first-child)').all()
    for row in rows:
        cols = await row.locator('div.col-md-3').all()
        if len(cols) >= 2:
            bin_type = await cols[0].inner_text()
            date_text = await cols[1].evaluate("node => Array.from(node.childNodes).filter(n => n.nodeType === 3).map(n => n.textContent).join('').trim()")
            bins.append({
                "type": bin_type.strip(),
                "collectionDate": date_text.strip()
            })
    
    await browser.close()
    return bins

data.notes: The scraper uses complex logic to extract the date from text nodes directly within a container to avoid nested paragraph tags. It also handles a cookie banner and uses keyboard navigation (TAB/ENTER) to submit forms.

async def run(playwright, postcode, house_number):
    browser = await playwright.chromium.launch(headless=True)
    context = await browser.new_context()
    page = await context.new_page()
    await page.goto('https://www.barnet.gov.uk/recycling-and-waste/bin-collections/find-your-bin-collection-day')

    # First cookie banner
    try:
        await page.click("button:has-text('Accept additional cookies')", timeout=5000)
    except:
        pass

    # Click link
    await page.click("text=Find your household collection day")

    # Second cookie banner
    try:
        await page.click("#epdagree", timeout=5000)
        await page.click("#epdsubmit", timeout=5000)
    except:
        pass

    # Enter postcode
    await page.fill('[aria-label="Postcode"]', postcode)
    await page.click('input[value="Find address"]')

    # Select address
    dropdown_selector = "select[id*='MainContent_CUSTOM_FIELD']"
    await page.wait_for_selector(dropdown_selector)
    options = await page.query_selector_all(f"{dropdown_selector} option")
    
    for option in options:
        text = await option.inner_text()
        if postcode.upper() in text.upper() and (f", {house_number}," in text or text.endswith(f", {house_number}")):
            val = await option.get_attribute('value')
            await page.select_option(dropdown_selector, val)
            break

    # Wait for results
    await page.wait_for_selector("text=Next collection date:", timeout=15000)
    content = await page.content()
    await browser.close()
    return content

data.notes: The scraper also fetches seasonal overrides from the main landing page to adjust collection dates during holidays.

from playwright.sync_api import sync_playwright
import datetime

def get_bin_data(uprn):
    with sync_playwright() as p:
        browser = p.chromium.launch(headless=True)
        page = browser.new_page()
        url = f"https://waste.bexley.gov.uk/waste/{uprn}"
        page.goto(url)
        
        # Wait for content to load
        page.wait_for_selector("xpath=/html/body/div[1]/div/div[2]/div")
        page.wait_for_selector("#loading-indicator", state="hidden")
        page.wait_for_timeout(3000)
        page.wait_for_selector(".waste-service-name")

        bins = []
        bin_sections = page.query_selector_all("h3.waste-service-name")
        for section in bin_sections:
            bin_type = section.inner_text().split('\n')[0].strip()
            # Locate the following summary list
            summary_list = section.evaluate_handle("node => node.nextElementSibling")
            while summary_list and await summary_list.evaluate("node => node.tagName !== 'DL'"):
                summary_list = summary_list.evaluate_handle("node => node.nextElementSibling")
            
            if summary_list:
                rows = summary_list.as_element().query_selector_all(".govuk-summary-list__row")
                for row in rows:
                    dt = row.query_selector("dt")
                    if dt and "Next collection" in dt.inner_text():
                        dd = row.query_selector("dd")
                        if dd:
                            date_text = dd.inner_text().strip()
                            bins.append({
                                "type": bin_type,
                                "collectionDate": datetime.datetime.strptime(date_text, "%A %d %B %Y").strftime("%d/%m/%Y")
                            })
        browser.close()
        return {"bins": bins}

data.notes: The scraper relies on a direct URL constructed with the UPRN. It handles dynamic content by waiting for a specific loading indicator to disappear and for the waste service elements to appear.

async def run(playwright, postcode, uprn):

    collection_link = await page.wait_for_selector("//a[contains(text(), 'Find Your Collection Day')]")

    await page.wait_for_selector('#addressSelect')

    await page.wait_for_selector("text=Your next collections", timeout=20000)

async def scrape_bin_collections(page, user_input):
    await page.goto("https://www.boston.gov.uk/findwastecollections")
    await page.wait_for_load_state("networkidle")
    
    try:
        await page.click("button[name='acceptall']", timeout=5000)
    except:
        pass

    await page.fill("#BBCWASTECOLLECTIONS_START_SEARCHPOSTCODE", user_input['postcode'])
    await page.click("#BBCWASTECOLLECTIONS_START_START10_NEXT")
    
    await page.wait_for_selector("#BBCWASTECOLLECTIONS_COLLECTIONADDRESS_INCIDENTUPRN_chosen")
    await page.click("#BBCWASTECOLLECTIONS_COLLECTIONADDRESS_INCIDENTUPRN_chosen")
    
    await page.wait_for_selector(".chosen-results")
    address_option = page.locator(f"//li[@class='active-result' and contains(text(), '{user_input['house_number']}')]")
    await address_option.click()
    
    await page.click("#BBCWASTECOLLECTIONS_COLLECTIONADDRESS_NEXT3_NEXT")
    await page.wait_for_selector("#BBCWASTECOLLECTIONS_SERVICE_FIELD859_OUTER")
    
    bins = []
    containers = await page.query_selector_all(".grid__cell.grid__cell--listitem.grid__cell--cols1")
    for container in containers:
        bin_type = await (await container.query_selector("h2.item__title")).inner_text()
        date_text = await (await container.query_selector(".item__content div")).inner_text()
        date_cleaned = date_text.replace("Next: ", "").strip()
        bins.append({
            "type": bin_type.strip(),
            "collectionDate": date_cleaned
        })
    return bins

from playwright.sync_api import sync_playwright
from datetime import datetime

def get_bin_data(postcode, house_number):
    with sync_playwright() as p:
        browser = p.chromium.launch(headless=True)
        page = browser.new_page()
        page.goto("https://enviroservices.brighton-hove.gov.uk/link/collections")
        
        # Search Postcode
        page.wait_for_selector(".form-control")
        page.fill(".form-control", postcode)
        page.click("//button[contains(@class, 'mx-name-actionButton3')]")
        
        # Select Address
        page.wait_for_selector(f'//option[contains(text(), "{house_number}")]')
        page.select_option("//select[contains(@class, 'form-control')]", label=house_number, index=None)
        
        # Submit selection
        page.click("//button[contains(@class, 'mx-name-actionButton5')]")
        
        # Wait for results list
        page.wait_for_selector(".mx-name-listView1")
        
        bins = []
        # Find all elements starting with mx-name-index-
        items = page.query_selector_all("[class*='mx-name-index-']")
        for item in items:
            bin_type = item.query_selector(".mx-name-text31").inner_text().strip()
            bin_date_str = item.query_selector(".mx-name-text29").inner_text().strip()
            # Format: 25 May 2024
            bin_date = datetime.strptime(bin_date_str, "%d %B %Y").strftime("%d/%m/%Y")
            bins.append({"type": bin_type, "collectionDate": bin_date})
        
        browser.close()
        return {"bins": bins}

data.notes: The scraper uses specific Mendix-generated class names (mx-name-...) which are likely stable across sessions but could change with site updates. The house_number is matched against the address text (PAON).

from playwright.sync_api import sync_playwright
from datetime import datetime
from dateutil.parser import parse
import re

def remove_ordinal(date_str):
    return re.sub(r'(\d+)(st|nd|rd|th)', r'\1', date_str)

def run(playwright, postcode, house_number, url):
    browser = playwright.chromium.launch(headless=True)
    page = browser.new_page()
    page.goto(url)

    # Handle Cookies
    try:
        page.click("#CybotCookiebotDialogBodyLevelButtonLevelOptinAllowAll", timeout=5000)
    except:
        pass

    # Enter Postcode
    page.fill("#Postcode", postcode)
    page.click("input[type='submit'].button.button--secondary")

    # Select Address
    page.wait_for_selector("#UprnAddress")
    options = page.query_selector_all("#UprnAddress option")
    found = False
    for option in options:
        text = option.inner_text().lower().strip()
        if text == house_number.lower().strip() or text.startswith(house_number.lower().strip() + " "):
            page.select_option("#UprnAddress", value=option.get_attribute("value"))
            found = True
            break
    
    if not found:
        raise Exception(f"Address {house_number} not found")

    # Submit Address Selection
    page.click("input[type='submit']")

    # Wait for results
    page.wait_for_selector("h4:has-text('Your next collections')")
    
    bins = []
    # Locate bin divs
    bin_divs = page.query_selector_all("div.card-body div.my-2")
    for div in bin_divs:
        strong = div.query_selector("strong")
        if strong:
            bin_type = strong.inner_text().replace(" (if applicable)", "").strip()
            full_text = div.inner_text().strip()
            date_text = full_text.replace(bin_type, "").strip()
            
            cleaned_date = remove_ordinal(date_text)
            parsed_date = parse(cleaned_date, fuzzy=True)
            
            bins.append({
                "type": bin_type,
                "collectionDate": parsed_date.strftime("%d/%m/%Y")
            })

    browser.close()
    return {"bins": bins}

with sync_playwright() as playwright:
    results = run(playwright, "NR10 3FD", "1 Park View", "https://area.southnorfolkandbroadland.gov.uk/FindAddress")
    print(results)

data.notes: The scraper uses fuzzy date parsing with dateutil to handle the varied date strings returned by the council portal. It converts ordinal dates (e.g., 21st) to standard format before parsing.

async def run(playwright, params):
    browser = await playwright.chromium.launch(headless=True)
    page = await browser.new_page()
    await page.goto(params['url'])
    await page.wait_for_selector('.waste-service-image', timeout=30000)
    
    results = []
    services = await page.query_selector_all('h3.govuk-heading-m.waste-service-name')
    for service in services:
        service_title = await service.inner_text()
        # Logic to find sibling summary list entries
        content_block = await page.evaluate_handle('(el) => el.nextElementSibling', service)
        next_coll_dt = await content_block.query_selector("dt:has-text('Next collection')")
        if next_coll_dt:
            next_coll_dd = await page.evaluate_handle('(el) => el.nextElementSibling', next_coll_dt)
            date_text = await next_coll_dd.inner_text()
            # Date cleaning logic would be handled in post-processing
            results.append({'type': service_title.strip(), 'collectionDate': date_text.strip()})
    
    await browser.close()
    return results

data.notes: The scraper relies on a direct URL to the results page, usually containing a specific ID or session token. The Python logic handles year-wrapping for dates that appear in the past relative to the current execution time.

Enter the postcode into the postal-code input field
Press Enter to trigger the address lookup
Select the specific address from the dropdown using the UPRN
Click the 'Next' button

playwright_workflow.code:
async def scrape_bins(page, user_postcode, user_uprn):
    await page.goto('https://www.broxbourne.gov.uk/bin-collection-date')
    # Handle Cloudflare / Page Load
    await page.wait_for_selector('input[autocomplete="postal-code"]', timeout=45000)
    
    # Cookie banner
    try:
        allow_all = page.get_by_role('button', name='Allow all')
        if await allow_all.is_visible():
            await allow_all.click()
    except: pass

    # Enter postcode
    postcode_input = page.locator('input[autocomplete="postal-code"]')
    await postcode_input.fill(user_postcode)
    await postcode_input.press('Enter')

    # Select UPRN
    address_select = page.locator('select')
    await address_select.wait_for(state='visible')
    await address_select.select_option(value=user_uprn)

    # Click Next
    await page.get_by_role('button', name='Next').click()

    # Wait for results
    await page.wait_for_selector('h1:has-text("When is my bin collection date?")')
    rows = await page.locator('h1:has-text("When is my bin collection date?") + table tr').all()
    
    results = []
    # Skip header row
    for row in rows[1:]:
        cols = await row.locator('td').all()
        if len(cols) >= 2:
            date_text = await cols[0].inner_text()
            bin_type = await cols[1].inner_text()
            results.append({"type": bin_type.strip(), "date": date_text.strip()})
    return results

data.notes: The scraper handles year-rollover logic where a date in January is assumed to be the following year if the current month is late in the year. It also specifically accounts for Cloudflare protection.

async def scrape_bins(page, user_input):
    await page.goto("https://selfservice.broxtowe.gov.uk/renderform.aspx?t=217&k=9D2EF214E144EE796430597FB475C3892C43C528")
    await page.fill("#ctl00_ContentPlaceHolder1_FF5683TB", user_input['postcode'])
    await page.click("#ctl00_ContentPlaceHolder1_FF5683BTN")
    await page.wait_for_selector("#ctl00_ContentPlaceHolder1_FF5683DDL")
    await page.select_option("#ctl00_ContentPlaceHolder1_FF5683DDL", value=f"U{user_input['uprn']}")
    await page.click("#ctl00_ContentPlaceHolder1_btnSubmit")
    await page.wait_for_selector("#ctl00_ContentPlaceHolder1_FF5686FormGroup table")
    
    bins = []
    rows = await page.query_selector_all("#ctl00_ContentPlaceHolder1_FF5686FormGroup table tr")
    for row in rows:
        cells = await row.query_selector_all("td")
        if len(cells) >= 4:
            bin_type = (await cells[0].inner_text()).strip()
            date_text = (await cells[3].inner_text()).strip()
            if bin_type and bin_type != "Bin Type" and date_text:
                bins.append({"type": bin_type, "collectionDate": date_text})
    return bins

[house_number] is compared against addressLine1 in the response list to find the correct property object id. Example logic: addr = next(item for item in response['response'] if house_number in item['addressLine1']) then property_id = addr['id'] used in step 2. The variable extracted below assumes the filtering is handled by the executor or a specific jsonpath if filtering is supported by the tool, but logically it is property_id from the filtered property record. Since PAON is passed as house_number in our inputs, we find that specific property's ID. Using jsonpath to select the ID from the response based on household filter would be logically: $.response[?(@.addressLine1 =~ '.*{house_number}.*')].id but for simplicity we list the variable extraction below as the result of that lookup step.  Original code uses PAON which corresponds to house_number in this schema context. Note: Bury's API expects postcode with no spaces as per the original code: {postcode}.replace(' ', ''). Result: ID of the selected property to use in Step 2. Extraction description: Extract the property id for the record where addressLine1 matches house_number. Variable Name: property_id.  Since simple jsonpath filters aren't always available in all implementations we define extraction as variable_name property_id extracted from the response json. Step 2 requires this value. Variable name: property_id. Extraction selector: $.response[?(@.addressLine1 =~ /.*{house_number}.*/)].id (logical). Actual extraction for step 2 will use this property_id variable. Result: property_id. Extractions list for this step follows. Note: the code strips space from postcode. Use {postcode_nospace} if implementing in environment with string manipulation, otherwise assuming standard {postcode} replacement. Description: Fetch list of properties for a postcode to extract property_id. Final extraction below for simplicity uses jsonpath selector for property id. Step 2 uses this property_id. [house_number] is used to filter property objects. This step returns a list of property objects each with an 'id'. Extraction: find the property where addressLine1 contains house_number and extract its id. Step 2 URL: https://www.bury.gov.uk/app-services/getPropertyById?id={property_id}. Extractions: property_id from $.response array filter by {house_number}.
Fetch bin collection data using the property ID retrieved in step 1.

async def run(playwright, postcode, house_number):
    browser = await playwright.chromium.launch(headless=True)
    page = await browser.new_page()
    await page.goto("https://www.chelmsford.gov.uk/bins-and-recycling/check-your-collection-day/")
    
    try:
        await page.click("xpath=//*[contains(text(), 'ACCEPT')]", timeout=5000)
    except:
        pass

    await page.fill("input[id*='_keyword']", postcode)
    await page.click(".__submitButton")
    await page.wait_for_selector("table")

    rows = await page.query_selector_all("tr")
    calendar_url = None
    for row in rows:
        text = await row.inner_text()
        if house_number in text:
            match = re.search(r"(Monday|Tuesday|Wednesday|Thursday|Friday)\s+([AB])", text, re.I)
            if match:
                day = match.group(1).lower()
                letter = match.group(2).lower()
                calendar_url = f"https://www.chelmsford.gov.uk/bins-and-recycling/check-your-collection-day/{day}-{letter}-collection-calendar/"
                break
    
    if calendar_url:
        await page.goto(calendar_url)
        ics_link = await page.get_attribute("a[href$='.ics']", "href")
        # Process ICS using icalevents or similar...
    
    await browser.close()

async def scrape_chichester(page, user_postcode, house_number):
    await page.goto('https://www.chichester.gov.uk/checkyourbinday')
    await page.fill('#WASTECOLLECTIONCALENDARV5_CALENDAR_ADDRESSLOOKUPPOSTCODE', user_postcode)
    await page.press('#WASTECOLLECTIONCALENDARV5_CALENDAR_ADDRESSLOOKUPSEARCH', 'Enter')
    
    dropdown_selector = '#WASTECOLLECTIONCALENDARV5_CALENDAR_ADDRESSLOOKUPADDRESS'
    await page.wait_for_selector(f'{dropdown_selector} option:nth-child(2)')
    
    options = await page.query_selector_all(f'{dropdown_selector} option')
    target_option = None
    user_input_clean = house_number.lower().strip()
    
    for option in options:
        text = (await option.inner_text()).lower().strip()
        if text == user_input_clean or text.startswith(f'{user_input_clean},'):
            target_option = option
            break
    
    if not target_option:
        for option in options:
            text = (await option.inner_text()).lower().strip()
            if user_input_clean in text:
                target_option = option
                break
                
    if target_option:
        await page.select_option(dropdown_selector, value=await target_option.get_attribute('value'))
    
    await page.wait_for_selector('.bin-collection-dates-container')
    rows = await page.query_selector_all('table.defaultgeneral.bin-collection-dates tr')
    
    bins = []
    for row in rows:
        cells = await row.query_selector_all('td')
        if len(cells) >= 2:
            date_text = await cells[0].inner_text()
            bin_type = await cells[1].inner_text()
            bins.append({'collectionDate': date_text.strip(), 'type': bin_type.strip()})
    return bins

data.notes: The scraper uses a robust address matching logic that attempts strict matching first, then falls back to fuzzy matching (contains house number).

async def run(playwright, postcode, uprn):
    browser = await playwright.chromium.launch(headless=True)
    context = await browser.new_context(user_agent="Mozilla/5.0 (Windows NT 10.0; Win64; x64) AppleWebKit/537.36 (KHTML, like Gecko) Chrome/120.0.0.0 Safari/537.36")
    page = await context.new_page()
    await page.goto("https://chorley.gov.uk/bincollectiondays")
    await page.click("//a[@class='button' and @href='https://forms.chorleysouthribble.gov.uk/chorley-bincollectiondays']")
    await page.wait_for_selector("//input[@type='text'][1]")
    await page.fill("//input[@type='text'][1]", postcode)
    await page.click("//button[contains(@class, 'btn--lookup')]")
    await page.wait_for_selector("//select[@class='form__select']")
    await page.wait_for_timeout(2000)
    options = await page.query_selector_all("//select[@class='form__select']/option")
    selected = False
    for i in range(1, len(options)):
        val = await options[i].get_attribute("value")
        if uprn in val or not selected:
            await page.select_option("//select[@class='form__select']", index=i)
            selected = True
            break
    await page.click("button[type='submit'][value='Next']")
    await page.wait_for_selector("//th[text()='Collection']")
    content = await page.content()
    await browser.close()
    return content

async def scrape_colchester(page, user_postcode, user_paon):
    await page.goto("https://www.colchester.gov.uk/your-recycling-calendar/?start=true")
    await page.click("#ccc-notify-accept")
    await page.wait_for_selector(".input-text")
    await page.fill(".input-text", user_postcode)
    await page.click(".button-small")
    await page.wait_for_selector(".input-select")
    await page.select_option(".input-select", label=f"{user_paon}")
    await page.click(".button-small")
    await page.wait_for_selector(".recycling-calendar")
    
    bins = []
    rows = await page.query_selector_all(".recycling-calendar-row")
    for row in rows:
        date_str = await (await row.query_selector("strong")).inner_text()
        items = await row.query_selector_all("li")
        for item in items:
            bin_type = await item.inner_text()
            bins.append({
                "type": bin_type.strip(),
                "collectionDate": date_str.strip()
            })
    return bins

async def run(playwright, house_number, postcode):
    browser = await playwright.chromium.launch(headless=True)
    page = await browser.new_page()
    await page.goto('https://community.cotswold.gov.uk/s/waste-collection-enquiry')
    
    # Wait for the Salesforce component to be ready
    await page.wait_for_timeout(10000)
    
    # Input address
    full_address = f"{house_number}, {postcode}" if house_number else postcode
    input_selector = "//label[contains(text(), 'Enter your address')]/following-sibling::*//input"
    await page.wait_for_selector(input_selector)
    await page.fill(input_selector, full_address)
    
    # Trigger and select dropdown
    await page.click(input_selector)
    await page.wait_for_selector("//li[@role='presentation']")
    await page.click("//li[@role='presentation']")
    
    # Submit
    await page.click("//button[contains(text(), 'Next')]")
    
    # Wait for results table
    await page.wait_for_selector("//span[contains(text(), 'Collection Day')]")
    
    content = await page.content()
    await browser.close()
    return content

steps: Navigate to the Croydon waste services address entry page.
Enter the user's postcode into the postcode input field.
Wait for the address selection dropdown to appear.
Select the address from the dropdown that contains the provided house number (PAON).
Click the 'Next' button to submit the address selection.
Wait for the collection results to load.
Extract bin types and collection dates from the resulting elements.
playwright_workflow.code:
async def run(playwright, postcode, house_number):
    browser = await playwright.chromium.launch(headless=True)
    context = await browser.new_context()
    page = await context.new_page()
    await page.goto('https://service.croydon.gov.uk/wasteservices/w/webpage/bin-day-enter-address')
    
    await page.fill('input[data-ts_identifier="postcode_input"]', postcode)
    await page.press('input[data-ts_identifier="postcode_input"]', 'Enter')
    
    await page.wait_for_selector('select[data-ts_identifier="address_selection"]')
    options = await page.query_selector_all('select[data-ts_identifier="address_selection"] option')
    
    address_value = None
    for option in options:
        text = await option.inner_text()
        if f' {house_number} ' in f' {text} ':
            address_value = await option.get_attribute('value')
            break
    
    if address_value:
        await page.select_option('select[data-ts_identifier="address_selection"]', address_value)
    else:
        raise Exception(f'House number {house_number} not found')
        
    await page.click('input[type="submit"][value="Next"]')
    await page.wait_for_selector('#mats_content_wrapper .listing_template_record')
    
    bin_data = []
    records = await page.query_selector_all('.listing_template_record')
    for record in records:
        bin_type_elem = await record.query_selector('h2')
        date_elem = await record.query_selector('span.value-as-text')
        if bin_type_elem and date_elem:
            bin_type = (await bin_type_elem.inner_text()).strip()
            date_str = (await date_elem.inner_text()).strip()
            bin_data.append({'type': bin_type, 'date': date_str})
            
    await browser.close()
    return bin_data

data.notes: The scraper uses a text search within the dropdown options to match the house number (PAON) accurately by wrapping both in spaces to avoid partial matches (e.g., matching '1' in '11').

api_steps.description: Fetch the bin collection schedule page directly using the UPRN

bin_extraction.date_selector: combination of preceding month name and following day number

data.notes: The scraper parses raw text content from the '.lgd-region--content' div. It identifies months (e.g., 'January'), then looks for day numbers (1-31). The line following a day number is treated as the bin type. It dynamically determines the year based on whether '2026' is mentioned in the text content, defaulting to the current year.

steps: Navigate to the Dacorum bin collection portal
Enter the postcode into the 'txtBxPCode' field
Click the 'Find Address' button
Select the address matching the house number from the 'lstBxAddrList' dropdown
Click the 'Get Schedules' button
Wait for the results to load and extract bin types and dates from the collection schedule panel
playwright_workflow.code:
async def scrape_bins(page, user_paon, user_postcode):
    await page.goto('https://webapps.dacorum.gov.uk/bincollections/')
    await page.wait_for_selector('#txtBxPCode')
    await page.fill('#txtBxPCode', user_postcode)
    await page.click('#btnFindAddr')
    
    # Select address containing the house number/PAON
    await page.wait_for_selector('#lstBxAddrList')
    await page.select_option('#lstBxAddrList', label={'contains': user_paon})
    
    await page.click('#MainContent_btnGetSchedules')
    await page.wait_for_selector('#lblSelectedAddr')
    
    bins = []
    # Find the collection schedule container
    container = await page.query_selector('#MainContent_updPnl')
    entries = await container.query_selector_all('div[style=" margin:5px;"]')
    
    for entry in entries:
        strong = await entry.query_selector('strong')
        if strong:
            bin_type = (await strong.inner_text()).strip()
            # Skip informational blocks
            if bin_type and not any(skip in bin_type.lower() for skip in ['please note', 'we may collect', 'bank holiday', 'different day']):
                date_cells = await entry.query_selector_all('div[style="display:table-cell;"]')
                if len(date_cells) > 1:
                    date_text = (await date_cells[1].inner_text()).strip()
                    if date_text:
                        # Date format: Tue, 01 Jan 2024
                        bins.append({
                            'type': bin_type,
                            'collectionDate': date_text
                        })
    return bins

data.notes: The scraper extracts data from a dynamic portal using house number matching within a dropdown. It filters out informational text blocks found in the same container style as actual collection entries.

steps: Navigate to the East Renfrewshire Council bin days page
Enter the postcode into the postal-code input field
Click the 'Search' button to retrieve addresses
Wait for the addresses dropdown to appear and select the address matching the UPRN or house number
Click the 'Find my collection dates' button
Wait for the results table containing 'Bin Type' to load
Extract bin types and collection dates from the table rows
playwright_workflow.code:
async def scrape_bins(page, user_postcode, user_paon=None, user_uprn=None):
    await page.goto('https://eastrenfrewshire.gov.uk/bin-days')
    await page.wait_for_selector('input[autocomplete="postal-code"]')
    await page.fill('input[autocomplete="postal-code"]', user_postcode)
    await page.click('button:has-text("Search")')
    
    address_select = await page.wait_for_selector('label:has-text("Addresses") + select')
    if user_uprn:
        await address_select.select_option(value=user_uprn)
    elif user_paon:
        options = await address_select.query_selector_all('option')
        for option in options:
            text = await option.inner_text()
            if user_paon.lower() in text.lower():
                await address_select.select_option(label=text)
                break
    else:
        await address_select.select_option(index=1)
    
    await page.click('button:has-text("Find my collection dates")')
    await page.wait_for_selector('th:has-text("Bin Type")')
    
    rows = await page.query_selector_all('table tr')
    bins = []
    for row in rows[1:]: # Skip header
        cells = await row.query_selector_all('td')
        if len(cells) >= 3:
            date_cell = (await cells[0].inner_text()).strip()
            bin_type_cell = await cells[2].inner_text()
            if date_cell:
                bin_types = [bt.strip() for bt in bin_type_cell.split('\n') if bt.strip()]
                for bin_type in bin_types:
                    bins.append({"type": bin_type, "collectionDate": date_cell})
    return bins

data.notes: The scraper handles multiple bin types listed in a single row by splitting on newlines. Dates are extracted in DD/MM/YYYY format and sorted chronologically.

async def run(playwright, postcode, house_number):
    browser = await playwright.chromium.launch(headless=True)
    context = await browser.new_context()
    page = await context.new_page()
    await page.goto('https://www.eastriding.gov.uk/environment/bins-rubbish-recycling/bins-and-collections/bin-collection-dates/#')
    
    try:
        await page.click('#er-cookie-placeholder-settings div a:first-child', timeout=5000)
    except:
        pass

    await page.click('a:has-text("Open all")')
    await page.fill('input[placeholder="Enter your postcode"]', postcode)
    await page.click('button:text("Search")')

    await page.wait_for_selector('select.dropdown')
    await page.select_option('select.dropdown', label=str(house_number))

    await page.wait_for_selector('.results')
    
    bin_data = []
    bin_types = {}
    headers = await page.query_selector_all('li[class*="header"]')
    for header in headers:
        text = await header.inner_text()
        classes = await header.get_attribute('class')
        type_class = [c for c in classes.split() if c != 'header'][0]
        bin_types[type_class] = text.strip()

    dates = await page.query_selector_all('li[class*="date"]')
    for date_el in dates:
        classes = await date_el.get_attribute('class')
        type_class = [c for c in classes.split() if c != 'date'][0]
        bin_type = bin_types.get(type_class)
        date_text = await (await date_el.query_selector('span')).inner_text()
        bin_data.append({"type": bin_type, "collectionDate": date_text.strip()})
    
    await browser.close()
    return bin_data

data.notes: The scraper uses the house_number to select the correct address from a dropdown list after searching by postcode. Date format in the response is '%a, %d %b %Y'.

async def scrape_east_suffolk(page, postcode, uprn):
    await page.goto('https://my.eastsuffolk.gov.uk/service/Bin_collection_dates_finder')
    iframe_element = await page.wait_for_selector('iframe#fillform-frame-1')
    frame = await iframe_element.content_frame()
    
    await frame.wait_for_selector('#alt_postcode_search')
    await frame.fill('#alt_postcode_search', postcode.replace(' ', ''))
    
    await frame.wait_for_selector('#alt_choose_address')
    await frame.wait_for_selector('.spinner-outer', state='hidden')
    await page.wait_for_timeout(2000)
    
    await frame.select_option('#alt_choose_address', value=uprn)
    await frame.wait_for_selector('.spinner-outer', state='hidden')
    await page.wait_for_timeout(2000)
    
    table_container = await frame.wait_for_selector('//div[@data-field-name="collection_details"]//div[contains(@class, "repeatable-table-wrapper")]')
    rows = await table_container.query_selector_all('table tbody tr')
    
    bins = []
    for row in rows:
        cols = await row.query_selector_all('td')
        if len(cols) >= 4:
            bin_type = (await (await cols[2].query_selector_all('span'))[1].inner_text()).strip().title()
            date_text = (await (await cols[3].query_selector_all('span'))[1].inner_text()).strip()
            bins.append({'type': bin_type, 'collectionDate': date_text})
    return bins

async def run(playwright, uprn):
    browser = await playwright.chromium.launch(headless=True)
    page = await browser.new_page()
    url = f'https://www.eastleigh.gov.uk/waste-bins-and-recycling/collection-dates/your-waste-bin-and-recycling-collections?uprn={uprn}'
    await page.goto(url)
    await page.wait_for_selector('.dl-horizontal')
    
    bins = []
    bin_types = [
        'Household Waste Bin',
        'Recycling Bin',
        'Food Waste Bin',
        'Glass Box and Batteries',
        'Garden Waste Bin'
    ]
    
    dl_elements = await page.query_selector_all('.dl-horizontal dt')
    dd_elements = await page.query_selector_all('.dl-horizontal dd')
    
    for dt, dd in zip(dl_elements, dd_elements):
        bin_type = (await dt.inner_text()).strip()
        date_text = (await dd.inner_text()).strip()
        
        if bin_type in bin_types and not date_text.startswith("You haven't yet signed up"):
            bins.append({
                'type': bin_type,
                'collectionDate': date_text
            })
    
    await browser.close()
    return bins

data.notes: The scraper uses a UPRN-based URL to directly access the results page. It filters based on a specific list of supported bin types and excludes those starting with 'You haven't yet signed up'.

calendar_pattern.pattern_description: Bins are collected on a fortnightly rota. User provides the 'collection_day' (e.g., Monday) and 'collection_week' (Week 1 or Week 2). Week 1 starts recycling/glass on 2025-11-03 and refuse on 2025-11-10. Week 2 swaps these. Collections are then calculated by adding an offset for the specific day of the week.
calendar_pattern.code_snippet:
collection_week_idx = ['Week 1', 'Week 2'].index(postcode)
days_of_week = ['Monday', 'Tuesday', 'Wednesday', 'Thursday', 'Friday', 'Saturday', 'Sunday']
offset_days = days_of_week.index(paon)
if collection_week_idx == 0:
    recycling_start = datetime(2025, 11, 3)
    refuse_start = datetime(2025, 11, 10)
else:
    recycling_start = datetime(2025, 11, 10)
    refuse_start = datetime(2025, 11, 3)
# Dates are then generated every 14 days from start + offset_days

data.notes: The script uses 'paon' to represent the collection day (e.g., Monday) and 'postcode' to represent the Week cycle (Week 1 or Week 2). This is a non-standard use of those input fields.

import re
from datetime import datetime
from playwright.sync_api import sync_playwright

def run(playwright, postcode, house_number):
    browser = playwright.chromium.launch(headless=True)
    context = browser.new_context(user_agent="Mozilla/5.0 (Windows NT 10.0; Win64; x64) AppleWebKit/537.36 (KHTML, like Gecko) Chrome/120.0.0.0 Safari/537.36")
    page = context.new_page()
    page.goto("https://www.enfield.gov.uk/services/rubbish-and-recycling/find-my-collection-day")

    # Wait for page to clear Cloudflare
    page.wait_for_load_state("networkidle")
    
    # Cookie rejection
    if page.locator("#ccc-notify-reject").is_visible():
        page.click("#ccc-notify-reject")

    # Find and switch to correct iframe if necessary
    frame = None
    for f in page.frames:
        if f.locator('[aria-label="Enter your address"]').count() > 0 or f.locator('input[placeholder*="postcode"]').count() > 0:
            frame = f
            break
    
    target = frame if frame else page
    
    # Enter Postcode
    target.fill('input[placeholder*="postcode"]', postcode)
    target.click("#submitButton0")

    # Wait for and Select Address
    target.wait_for_selector('[aria-label="Select full address"]')
    select_handle = target.locator('[aria-label="Select full address"]')
    
    options = select_handle.locator("option").all()
    target_value = ""
    for opt in options:
        text = opt.inner_text()
        if text.startswith(house_number):
            target_value = opt.get_attribute("value")
            break
    
    if target_value:
        select_handle.select_option(value=target_value)
    
    # Wait for results
    target.wait_for_selector("#FinalResults")
    html = target.inner_html("#FinalResults")
    
    # Parse results similar to BeautifulSoup logic
    bins = []
    # Simple regex extraction from the results HTML
    date_pattern = r"\b\d{2}/\d{2}/\d{4}\b"
    type_pattern = r"Your next (.*?) collection"
    
    # In a real scenario, we'd split the HTML by entry divs
    # This is a simplified representation of the logic
    matches = re.finditer(type_pattern, html)
    for match in matches:
        bin_type = match.group(1)
        # Seek date following the bin type
        date_match = re.search(date_pattern, html[match.end():])
        if date_match:
            bins.append({"type": bin_type, "date": date_match.group(0)})
    
    browser.close()
    return bins

data.notes: The site uses an iframe to host the bin lookup form and has Cloudflare protection. The scraper uses logic to find the specific iframe containing the 'Enter your address' label before interacting.

async def run(playwright, postcode):
    browser = await playwright.chromium.launch(headless=True)
    page = await browser.new_page()
    url = f"https://eppingforestdc.maps.arcgis.com/apps/instant/lookup/index.html?appid=bfca32b46e2a47cd9c0a84f2d8cdde17&find={postcode}"
    await page.goto(url)
    
    # Wait for loader to disappear
    await page.wait_for_selector(".esri-widget--loader-container", state="hidden")
    
    # Wait for content to be visible
    content_selector = ".esri-feature-content"
    await page.wait_for_selector(content_selector, state="visible", timeout=20000)
    
    # Get content for parsing
    paragraphs = await page.query_selector_all(f"{content_selector} p")
    bins = []
    for p in paragraphs:
        text = await p.inner_text()
        if "collection day is" in text:
            parts = text.split(" collection day is ")
            bin_type = parts[0].strip()
            date_str = parts[1].strip()
            bins.append({"type": bin_type, "collectionDate": date_str})
    
    await browser.close()
    return bins

data.notes: The scraper uses an ArcGIS Instant Lookup app. The postcode is passed directly in the URL query string, and data is extracted from the dynamically loaded feature content sidebar.

async def run(playwright, uprn, postcode):
    browser = await playwright.chromium.launch(headless=True)
    page = await browser.new_page()
    await page.goto('https://iportal.itouchvision.com/icollectionday/collection-day/?uuid=8E7DCC4BD90D8405D154BE053147018A8C0B5F09')
    await page.wait_for_selector('#postcodeSearch')
    await page.evaluate('(postcode) => { const input = document.getElementById("postcodeSearch"); const nativeInputValueSetter = Object.getOwnPropertyDescriptor(window.HTMLInputElement.prototype, "value").set; nativeInputValueSetter.call(input, postcode); input.dispatchEvent(new Event("input", { bubbles: true })); input.dispatchEvent(new Event("change", { bubbles: true })); }', postcode)
    await page.click('.govuk-button')
    await page.wait_for_selector('#addressSelect')
    await page.evaluate('(uprn) => { const select = document.getElementById("addressSelect"); select.value = uprn; select.dispatchEvent(new Event("change", { bubbles: true })); }', uprn)
    await page.wait_for_selector('h3')
    content = await page.content()
    await browser.close()
    return content

data.notes: The portal uses React, necessitating the use of native value setters and manual event dispatching in JavaScript to ensure the internal state updates when the postcode and UPRN are set. Dates are provided in 'Day DD Month' format and require year inference.

async def scrape_fife_bins(page, postcode, house_number):
    url = "https://fife.portal.uk.empro.verintcloudservices.com/site/fife/request/bin_calendar"
    await page.goto(url)
    
    # Wait for cloudflare or initial load
    await page.wait_for_load_state('networkidle')
    
    postcode_input = "#dform_widget_ps_45M3LET8_txt_postcode"
    search_btn = "#dform_widget_ps_3SHSN93_searchbutton"
    address_select = "#dform_widget_ps_3SHSN93_id"
    collections_table = "#dform_table_tab_collections"

    await page.wait_for_selector(postcode_input)
    await page.fill(postcode_input, postcode)
    await page.click(search_btn)

    await page.wait_for_selector(f"{address_select} option:nth-child(2)")
    
    # Find and select the correct address
    options = await page.query_selector_all(f"{address_select} option")
    paon_norm = str(house_number).strip().lower()
    for option in options:
        text = (await option.inner_text()).strip().lower()
        if paon_norm in text:
            await page.select_option(address_select, label=await option.inner_text())
            break

    await page.wait_for_selector(f"{collections_table} .dform_tr")
    
    # Extract data
    bins = []
    rows = await page.query_selector_all(f"{collections_table} .dform_tr")
    # Skip header
    for row in rows[1:]:
        cells = await row.query_selector_all(".dform_td")
        if len(cells) >= 2:
            img = await cells[0].query_selector("img")
            bin_type = await img.get_attribute("alt") if img else None
            date_text = await cells[1].inner_text()
            bins.append({
                "type": bin_type.strip() if bin_type else None,
                "collectionDate": date_text.strip()
            })
    return bins

data.notes: The council uses a Verint/Empro portal. The bin type is specifically found in the 'alt' attribute of an image in the first column of the collections table. Dates are provided in a long-form format (e.g., Wednesday, November 12, 2025).

async def run(playwright):
    browser = await playwright.chromium.launch(headless=True)
    context = await browser.new_context()
    page = await context.new_page()
    full_address = f"{house_number}, {postcode}"
    await page.goto("https://community.fdean.gov.uk/s/waste-collection-enquiry")
    
    search_input = await page.wait_for_selector('//*[@placeholder="Search Properties..."]')
    await search_input.fill(full_address)
    
    address_suggestion = await page.wait_for_selector(f'//*[@title="{full_address}"]', state="visible")
    await address_suggestion.click()
    
    next_button = await page.wait_for_selector("//lightning-button/button[contains(text(), 'Next')]")
    await next_button.click()
    
    table = await page.wait_for_selector('//table[@class="slds-table slds-table_header-fixed slds-table_bordered slds-table_edit slds-table_resizable-cols"]')
    rows = await table.query_selector_all('tr.slds-hint-parent')
    
    bins = []
    for row in rows:
        bin_type_cell = await row.query_selector('th')
        date_cell = await row.query_selector('td')
        if bin_type_cell and date_cell:
            bin_type = await bin_type_cell.get_attribute('data-cell-value')
            date_text = await date_cell.get_attribute('data-cell-value')
            bins.append({"type": bin_type.strip(), "collectionDate": date_text.strip()})
    
    await browser.close()
    return bins

playwright_workflow.code:
from playwright.sync_api import sync_playwright
from datetime import datetime

def run(playwright, postcode, house_number):
    browser = playwright.chromium.launch(headless=True)
    page = browser.new_page()
    page.goto('https://my.guildford.gov.uk/customers/s/view-bin-collections')
    
    # Enter postcode
    input_selector = "//input[contains(@class, 'slds-input')]"
    page.wait_for_selector(input_selector)
    page.fill(input_selector, postcode)
    page.keyboard.press('Enter')
    
    # Select address
    address_xpath = f"//lightning-base-formatted-text[contains(text(), '{house_number}')]"
    page.wait_for_selector(address_xpath)
    # Find the radio button relative to the text
    radio_xpath = f"{address_xpath}/../../../../preceding-sibling::td//input[@type='radio']"
    page.click(radio_xpath)
    
    # Click Next
    page.click("//button[contains(@name,'NEXT')]")
    
    # Wait for results
    page.wait_for_selector(".cBinScheduleDisplay")
    page.wait_for_selector('//div[contains(@title,"Bin Job")]')
    
    # Extract data
    rows = page.query_selector_all("tr.slds-hint-parent")
    bins = []
    for row in rows:
        bin_type_el = row.query_selector("td[data-label='Bin Job'] strong")
        date_el = row.query_selector("td[data-label='Next Collection']")
        
        if bin_type_el and date_el:
            bin_type = bin_type_el.inner_text().strip()
            date_raw = date_el.inner_text().strip()
            
            # Date Parsing logic
            try:
                parsed_date = datetime.strptime(date_raw, "%A, %d %B")
                current_date = datetime.now()
                year = current_date.year + 1 if parsed_date.month < current_date.month else current_date.year
                formatted_date = parsed_date.replace(year=year).strftime("%d/%m/%Y")
                bins.append({"type": bin_type, "collectionDate": formatted_date})
            except ValueError:
                continue
    
    browser.close()
    return {"bins": bins}

data.notes: The scraper uses a specific relative XPATH to find the radio button based on the house number text inside a Lightning component table structure. The year is inferred based on the current month.

    # Step 1: Get Calendar Rotation
    await page.goto('https://www.hertsmere.gov.uk/Environment-Refuse-and-Recycling/Recycling--Waste/Bin-collections/Collections-and-calendar.aspx')
    # Logic to extract 'current' week and bin mappings would go here using page.content()

async def run(playwright, postcode, house_number):
    browser = await playwright.chromium.launch(headless=True)
    page = await browser.new_page()
    await page.goto('https://hillingdon.gov.uk/check-your-collection-day')

    # Handle cookie banner
    try:
        await page.click('button.btn.btn--cookiemessage.btn--cancel.btn--contrast', timeout=5000)
    except:
        pass

    # Enter postcode
    await page.fill('#WASTECOLLECTIONDAYLOOKUPINCLUDEGARDEN_ADDRESSLOOKUPPOSTCODE', postcode)
    await page.press('#WASTECOLLECTIONDAYLOOKUPINCLUDEGARDEN_ADDRESSLOOKUPPOSTCODE', 'Enter')

    # Wait for address dropdown and select matching house number
    await page.wait_for_selector('#WASTECOLLECTIONDAYLOOKUPINCLUDEGARDEN_ADDRESSLOOKUPADDRESS option')
    options = await page.query_selector_all('#WASTECOLLECTIONDAYLOOKUPINCLUDEGARDEN_ADDRESSLOOKUPADDRESS option')
    for option in options:
        text = await option.inner_text()
        if house_number.lower() in ''.join(filter(str.isalnum, text.lower())):
            await option.select_option()
            break

    # Wait for data table
    await page.wait_for_selector('#WASTECOLLECTIONDAYLOOKUPINCLUDEGARDEN_COLLECTIONTABLE')
    content = await page.content()
    
    # Check bank holidays if needed
    await page.goto('https://www.hillingdon.gov.uk/bank-holiday-collections')
    holiday_content = await page.content()
    
    await browser.close()
    return content, holiday_content

async def scrape_bins(page, user_postcode, user_uprn):
    await page.goto("https://www.horsham.gov.uk/waste-recycling-and-bins/household-bin-collections/check-your-bin-collection-day")
    try:
        await page.click("button#ccc-notify-accept", timeout=5000)
    except:
        pass
    await page.fill("//input[@value='Enter your postcode']", user_postcode)
    await page.click("#Submit1")
    await page.wait_for_selector("select:has(option:has-text('Please select address...'))")
    await page.select_option("select:has(option:has-text('Please select address...'))", value=user_uprn)
    await page.wait_for_selector("//th[contains(text(),'COLLECTION TYPE')]/ancestor::table")
    rows = await page.query_selector_all("table tbody tr")
    bins = []
    for row in rows:
        cells = await row.query_selector_all("td")
        if len(cells) >= 3:
            date_str = await cells[1].inner_text()
            bin_type = await cells[2].inner_text()
            bins.append({"type": bin_type.strip(), "collectionDate": date_str.strip()})
    return bins

api_steps.description: Initial landing page to start session and find the entry point link (seq=1). icon/link extraction used for identifying the correct form URL segment if needed, though session tracking is the primary goal here. The scraper identifies the 'portal_link' from the response body containing 'seq=1'. Since the implementation uses a Session object, cookies are handled automatically across steps 1-4. For simplicity, we extract the seq=1 URL to proceed to step 2 as required by the code flow which parses all links. Note: the actual code logic extracts the link containing 'seq=1' from this response and uses it in step 2. We represent this via a regex or css selector extraction. Given the input code parses 'a' tags, we'll use a regex on the body or a css selector with attribute contains. In this case, we'll define extraction for the link with seq=1 which contains the token/session identifier in the URL path for the session start. The logic implies getting the href from a link with seq=1. Let's use a regex to capture it for use in the next step's URL. However, the code uses 'portal_link' as a full URL from the 'a' tag. We'll simulate this with a css selector and attribute extraction. In actual Whitespace systems, step 1 sets the session cookie and provides the entry point for the address search form. We'll use a simplified flow where we get the link for the form page from step 1 and then use it in step 2. Note: Whitespace systems usually require specific seq values in the URL to maintain state (seq=1 is search, seq=2 is search result, seq=3 is schedule). This fits the 'id_lookup_then_api' pattern as we look up the address 'id' link before getting data. The extraction here represents capturing the search form URL.

api_steps.description: Submit address search and identify the link to the collection schedule (seq=3)
Fetch the final page containing the bin collection dates

data.notes: Lancaster City Council uses the Whitespace (Web Receptacle Portal) system. The scraper follows a sequential URL pattern (seq=1, seq=3) to maintain state within a session. The bin extraction logic in the code iterates through list items in groups of 3 where the second element is the date and the third element (or a child of it) is the bin type.

steps: Navigate to the Leeds bin check page
Wait for the postcode input field to be clickable
Enter the user's postcode into the input field
Click the 'Look up Address' button using the Enter key
Wait for the address selection dropdown to appear
Select the address matching the user's UPRN from the dropdown
Wait for the bin collection information container to load
Extract the bin types and collection dates from the resulting HTML
playwright_workflow.code:
async def run(playwright, user_postcode, user_uprn):
    browser = await playwright.chromium.launch(headless=True)
    page = await browser.new_page()
    await page.goto("https://www.leeds.gov.uk/residents/bins-and-recycling/check-your-bin-day")
    
    await page.wait_for_selector("#postcode")
    await page.fill("#postcode", user_postcode)
    await page.press("button:has-text('Look up Address')", "Enter")
    
    dropdown_selector = 'select:has(option:has-text("Select an address"))'
    await page.wait_for_selector(dropdown_selector)
    await page.select_option(dropdown_selector, value=user_uprn)
    
    await page.wait_for_selector("div.lcc-bins")
    content = await page.content()
    # Process HTML with BeautifulSoup as per original script logic
    await browser.close()

data.notes: The scraper uses the UPRN specifically to select the correct address from the dropdown after a postcode search. It filters out calendar-specific bin elements and parses dates in the format '%A %d %b %Y'.

api_steps.description: Post the UPRN to the Biffa Leicester AJAX endpoint to retrieve collection dates.

data.notes: The UPRN is padded to 12 digits using zfill(12) before being sent in the payload. The API response contains an array of collection objects under the 'anyType' key.

api_steps.description: Fetch the collection schedule page using the UPRN

data.notes:
The scraper extracts Rubbish from the 3rd

tag, Recycling from the 5th, and Garden waste from the 7th. It uses an ordinal indicator removal function on dates. The logic handles backwards compatibility for older URL-based configurations if UPRN is not provided.

api_steps.description: Fetch the bin collection dates page using the provided UPRN

data.notes: The scraper uses a regex to strip ordinal suffixes (st, nd, rd, th) from the date string before parsing. It also handles year rollover logic where if the current month is late in the year and the extracted date is early in the year, it increments the year.

api_steps.description: Fetch the bin collection table for the given UPRN

data.notes: The scraper handles 'Today' and 'Tomorrow' strings and handles year rollover logic when the current month is December but the collection is in January. It removes ordinal indicators (st, nd, rd, th) before parsing.

api_steps.description: Fetch bin collection schedule directly from the Ealing custom script endpoint using UPRN.

data.notes: The API returns collection data in a list under the 'param2' key of the JSON response.

api_steps.description: Fetch bin collection data for the provided UPRN using a public AJAX endpoint.

data.notes: The endpoint uses a static parameter 'r=12345' in the URL which appears to be a cache-buster or generic request ID. The date extraction splits the 'eventTime' string at the 'T' character to remove the time component before parsing.

api_steps.description: Fetch bin collection schedule using UPRN from the council's API

data.notes: The API requires a subscription key and a POST body containing the UPRN and a start date. The date format in the response includes time, which is handled by the %d/%m/%Y %H:%M:%S format string.

data.notes: The scraper includes logic to handle commercial bins by checking if 'Recycling' is in the Container Name to differentiate between recycling and refuse. For standard residential bins, it uses the DisplayPhrase directly.

steps: Navigate to the Lewisham bin collection page
Accept cookies if the consent banner appears
Wait for the address finder to load and enter the user postcode into the input field
Click the 'Find address' button
Wait for the address dropdown to populate and select the address matching the user UPRN
Wait for the results page to load and extract bin types and collection dates
playwright_workflow.code:
async def scrape_lewisham(page, postcode, uprn):
    await page.goto('https://lewisham.gov.uk/myservices/recycling-and-rubbish/your-bins/collection')
    try:
        await page.click('#CybotCookiebotDialogBodyLevelButtonLevelOptinAllowAll', timeout=5000)
    except:
        pass
    await page.wait_for_selector('.address-finder')
    await page.fill('.js-address-finder-input', postcode)
    await page.click('.js-address-finder-step-address')
    await page.wait_for_selector('#address-selector > option:nth-child(2)')
    await page.select_option('#address-selector', value=uprn)
    await page.wait_for_url('**/find-your-collection-day-result')
    
    bins = []
    container = await page.query_selector('.js-find-collection-result')
    strong_tags = await container.query_selector_all('strong')
    for strong in strong_tags:
        bin_type = (await strong.inner_text()).strip()
        # The schedule text is in a sibling div/span; Lewisham structure uses multiple siblings
        parent_text = await page.evaluate('(node) => node.parentElement.innerText', strong)
        # Use regex on the parent text to find relevant date for this bin type
        import re
        date_match = re.search(r'Your next collection date is\s*(\d{2}/\d{2}/\d{4})', parent_text)
        if date_match:
            bins.append({
                'type': bin_type,
                'collectionDate': date_match.group(1)
            })
    return bins

data.notes: The scraper uses a combination of postcode lookup and UPRN selection from a dropdown. Dates are parsed from a string using regex pattern 'Your next collection date is DD/MM/YYYY'.

async def scrape(page, house_number):
    await page.goto('https://www.richmond.gov.uk/services/waste_and_recycling/bin_collection_day_search')
    # Dismiss cookie banner
    try:
        await page.click('#ccc-dismiss-button', timeout=5000)
    except:
        pass
    # Input street/house
    await page.fill('#Street', house_number)
    await page.click('div#maincontent input[type="submit"]')
    # Wait for table
    await page.wait_for_selector('div#maincontent table')
    # Extract headers and data
    headers = await page.query_selector_all('div#maincontent table tr:nth-child(1) th')
    data_cells = await page.query_selector_all('div#maincontent table tr:nth-child(2) td')
    
    bins = []
    for i in range(2, len(headers)):
        bin_type = await headers[i].inner_text()
        date_text = await data_cells[i].inner_text()
        bins.append({"type": bin_type.strip(), "date": date_text.strip()})
    return bins

async def run(playwright, user_postcode, user_paon):
    browser = await playwright.chromium.launch(headless=True)
    page = await browser.new_page()
    await page.goto("https://my.maidstone.gov.uk/service/Find-your-bin-day")
    
    # Handle iframe
    frame_element = await page.wait_for_selector("#fillform-frame-1")
    frame = await frame_element.content_frame()
    
    # Postcode search
    await frame.fill("input[name='postcode']", user_postcode)
    await frame.press("input[name='postcode']", "Tab")
    await frame.press("input[name='postcode']", "ArrowDown")
    
    # Select address
    address_selector = f"//div[contains(text(), ' {user_paon}')]"
    await frame.click(address_selector)
    
    # Wait for results
    await frame.wait_for_selector("div.col-collection-panel:has-text('Next collection')")
    
    # Extract data
    bins = []
    panels = await frame.query_selector_all("div.col-collection-panel")
    for panel in panels:
        bin_type = (await (await panel.query_selector("h3.collectionDataHeader")).inner_text()).strip()
        lines = await panel.query_selector_all("ul li")
        for line in lines:
            text = await line.inner_text()
            if "Next collection" in text:
                date_str = text.split("Next collection")[1].strip(": ").strip()
                bins.append({"type": bin_type, "collectionDate": date_str})
    
    await browser.close()
    return bins

data.notes: The site uses a nested iframe structure and requires interaction with a dynamic dropdown to populate results.

calendar_pattern.pattern_description: Collections occur every 14 days (bi-weekly) based on reference dates for Refuse and Recycling. The specific collection day is offset from these reference dates based on user input. Dates are adjusted for bank holidays and weekends using a cascading logic (moving to the next valid weekday, with major holidays potentially causing longer delays). Garden collections are bi-weekly but suspended during the Christmas/New Year period.

data.notes: The council script repurposes standard input fields: 'house_number' (via paon) is used for the collection day of the week, 'postcode' is used for the garden collection week (Week 1 or Week 2), and 'uprn' is used for the garden collection day of the week. Garden waste is skipped if the date falls between Dec 23rd and Jan 3rd.

async def scrape_bin_data(page, user_input):
    await page.goto("https://beta.npt.gov.uk/bins-and-recycling/bin-day-finder/")
    await page.click("#ccc-notify-accept")
    await page.fill("#PostCode", user_input['postcode'])
    await page.click("button:has-text('Find address')")
    await page.wait_for_selector("#Address")
    await page.select_option("#Address", label=f"{user_input['house_number']}")
    await page.click("input[value='Find bin day']")
    await page.wait_for_selector("#contentInner")
    
    bins = []
    dates = await page.query_selector_all("#contentInner .umb-block-grid__layout-item h2")
    for date_header in dates:
        date_text = await date_header.inner_text()
        if "Bank Holidays" in date_text: continue
        
        # Process date (logic similar to BeautifulSoup in Selenium snippet)
        clean_date = date_text.replace("(Today)", "").replace("(Tomorrow)", "").strip()
        
        # Find siblings for bin types
        bin_wrappers = await date_header.evaluate_handle('node => node.nextElementSibling.querySelectorAll(".card-body")')
        for wrapper in await bin_wrappers.get_properties():
            bin_type_el = await wrapper.as_element().query_selector("a")
            bin_info_el = await wrapper.as_element().query_selector("span")
            if bin_type_el and bin_info_el:
                bin_type = f"{await bin_type_el.inner_text()} ({await bin_info_el.inner_text()})"
                bins.append({"type": bin_type.strip(), "collectionDate": clean_date})
    return bins

data.notes: The scraper extracts dates from h2 elements and then searches for subsequent div containers with the class 'card-body' to find the specific bin types associated with that date.

playwright_workflow.code:
async def run(playwright, postcode, uprn):
    browser = await playwright.chromium.launch(headless=True)
    context = await browser.new_context(user_agent="Mozilla/5.0 (iPhone; CPU iPhone OS 15_4 like Mac OS X) AppleWebKit/605.1.15 (KHTML, like Gecko) CriOS/101.0.4951.44 Mobile/15E148 Safari/604.1")
    page = await context.new_page()
    await page.goto("https://forms.newforest.gov.uk/ufs/FIND_MY_BIN_BAR.eb")
    await page.reload()
    
    postcode_input = await page.wait_for_selector('//input[@id="CTID-JmLqCKl2-_-A"]')
    await postcode_input.scroll_into_view_if_needed()
    await page.evaluate(f"(el) => el.value = '{postcode}'", postcode_input)
    
    await page.click('//input[@type="submit"]')
    
    dropdown = await page.wait_for_selector('//select[@id="CTID-KOeKcmrC-_-A"]')
    await dropdown.select_option(value=uprn)
    
    await page.click('//input[@value="Submit"]')
    await page.wait_for_timeout(2000)

    legacy_link = await page.query_selector('//a[contains(text(),"Find your current bin collection day")]')
    bins = []
    if legacy_link:
        await legacy_link.click()
        await page.wait_for_timeout(2000)
        # Legacy parsing logic would go here (BeautifulSoup equivalent)
    else:
        table_rows = await page.wait_for_selector('//table[contains(@class,"eb-1j4UaesZ-tableContent")]//tr[contains(@class,"eb-1j4UaesZ-tableRow")]')
        rows = await page.query_selector_all('.eb-1j4UaesZ-tableRow')
        for row in rows:
            cols = await row.query_selector_all('td')
            if len(cols) >= 2:
                date_text = await cols[0].inner_text()
                bin_type = await cols[1].inner_text()
                bins.append({"type": bin_type.strip(), "collectionDate": date_text.strip()})
    
    await browser.close()
    return bins

data.notes: The council uses a dynamic form system (Enabling Enterprise/UFS). It specifically requires a page refresh after the initial load to prevent redirect issues and uses custom mobile user agents for better compatibility. There is a fallback 'legacy' view if the standard table is not present.

async def run(playwright, postcode, house_number):
    browser = await playwright.chromium.launch(headless=True)
    context = await browser.new_context()
    page = await context.new_page()
    await page.goto("https://forms.north-norfolk.gov.uk/outreach/BinCollectionDays.ofml")
    await page.fill("input#F_Address_subform\\:Postcode", postcode)
    await page.click("input#BA_Address_subform\\:Search_button")
    await page.wait_for_selector("select#F_Address_subform\\:Id")
    options = await page.query_selector_all("select#F_Address_subform\\:Id option")
    for option in options:
        text = await option.inner_text()
        if house_number.lower() in text.lower():
            await option.click()
            break
    await page.wait_for_selector("div[class*='fieldmergedcolumn'] ul")
    content = await page.content()
    # Processing would continue here to parse the HTML via soup or regex as in the original script
    await browser.close()
